prompt: A dramatic vertical cinematic poster for the dark wuxia action game Phantom Blade Zero, painted in an ultra-detailed gritty concept-art style with realistic textures, moody volumetric fog, and high contrast lighting. The scene shows 1 lone swordsman standing in the lower center foreground, viewed mostly from behind in a three-quarter pose, wearing a wide straw hat and layered black robes shredded into long wind-whipped strips, with pale loose hair streaming out beneath the hat. He holds 1 drawn sword low in his right hand, the blade catching cold silver light. The environment is a ruined ancient Chinese city of towering wooden pagodas and fortress-like buildings rising into steep cliffs, with collapsing architecture, hanging chains, smoke, drifting ash, and rubble. A bright full moon glows near the top center behind dense storm clouds, backlighting the skyline and the warrior. The color palette is almost monochrome charcoal, black, steel gray, and moonlit white, punctuated by sharp crimson slashes and blood-like streaks cutting diagonally across the composition. At the very top, place 4 huge Chinese calligraphy characters in explosive white brushstroke ink reading 影之刃零, spanning nearly the full width. Directly beneath, centered, add the small elegant serif English subtitle PHANTOM BLADE ZERO. At the bottom center, add 1 line of Chinese tagline text reading 刀光即命运 影尽方归零, with the final character in red, plus 1 smaller line of Chinese promotional text beneath it. Add subtle ornamental divider lines and a tiny red seal-like emblem at the bottom. Composition should feel like an official AAA key art poster: epic, ominous, martial, supernatural, and intensely atmospheric, with dynamic wind, debris, embers, torn fabric, and ink-splatter energy.
